title: Q Movie reactions NO SPOILER
wikitext:
[]: Q Movie reactions (NO SPOILER) Post by Sik » Fri Nov 16, 2012 7:25 pm Hello I made this post so people who went to the movies can share with us (the ones not living in Japan :P) about the experiences they might have. Attention: I dont intend you to write about the movie itself, just wanna know how full the theater was, how excited the people surely was, if you camped in advance, anecdotes, extrange stuff, presentations, etc. :)!

[]: Post by Stan » Fri Nov 16, 2012 8:51 pm Yeah Im also looking forward to hear from our fellow evageeks of their impressions and the overall audience impression of the film. From what I read in the spoiler section, I think lots of minds will be blown that night [might get messy]

[]: Post by riffraff11235 » Fri Nov 16, 2012 9:14 pm Ornette will be going to see the movie soon. I'll be waiting on his spoilerless review!

[]: Post by AR-99 » Fri Nov 16, 2012 10:11 pm I wonder if anyone cosplayed to the first screening like they would do in the US.

[]: Post by zabrewolf » Fri Nov 16, 2012 10:42 pm this

[]: Post by ancapevageek » Fri Nov 16, 2012 10:48 pm so it's roughly 12:45 pm over there. How long until the first screenings begin, and confirmations of these spoilers start rolling?

[]: Post by Chrad » Fri Nov 16, 2012 10:51 pm ancapevageek wrote: so it's roughly 12:45 pm over there. How long until the first screenings begin, and confirmations of these spoilers start rolling?

[]: Post by ancapevageek » Fri Nov 16, 2012 11:05 pm Well then, that's awkward.

[]: Post by riffraff11235 » Fri Nov 16, 2012 11:05 pm ancapevageek wrote: so it's roughly 12:45 pm over there. How long until the first screenings begin, and confirmations of these spoilers start rolling?

[]: Post by Warren Peace » Fri Nov 16, 2012 11:21 pm Stan wrote: Yeah Im also looking forward to hear from our fellow evageeks of their impressions and the overall audience impression of the film. From what I read in the spoiler section, I think lots of minds will be blown that night [might get messy]

[]: Post by laika » Sat Nov 17, 2012 2:19 am I'll be seeing it on Tuesday, so I'll be sure to write my impressions here. I've lurked this forum for years, but I figured now's as good a time as any to actually join. よろしくおねがいします！

[]: i loved it Post by michaelovan » Sat Nov 17, 2012 11:58 am from the first frame, the film went in a completely unexpected direction. it had a different vibe to it than anything eva-related that has come before - and i'm still processing that change. the themes are all there. the characters are true to the characters. but the locations, the timeframe, the music... there were some surprises, to say the least. before 3.0, there's a short film from hideaki anno and studio ghibli. it's pretty awesome. just to be clear i loved the film. i can't wait to see it again. oh yes, this was my first post. long time voyeur, first time poster. i was so excited after the film that i had to start an account just so i could talk about it.

[]: Re: i loved it Post by Xard » Sat Nov 17, 2012 12:01 pm michaelovan wrote: the themes are all there. the characters are true to the characters.

[]: Re: i loved it Post by michaelovan » Sat Nov 17, 2012 12:15 pm Xard wrote: overreacting to cosmetics of storyline

[]: Post by Hyper Shinchan » Sat Nov 17, 2012 12:24 pm ^ Probably there were some Americans in the theatre, they started clapping and the Japanese followed in suit. It's the only possible explanation.

[]: Post by Xard » Sat Nov 17, 2012 12:28 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: ^ Probably there were some Americans in the theatre, they started clapping and the Japanese followed in suit. It's the only possible explanation.

[]: Post by michaelovan » Sat Nov 17, 2012 12:35 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: ^ Probably there were some Americans in the theatre, they started clapping

[]: Post by Der Kommissar » Sat Nov 17, 2012 1:14 pm We're gonna blame us Americlaps for clapping in Japan? That's going too far.

[]: Post by Xard » Sat Nov 17, 2012 1:18 pm Der Kommissar wrote: We're gonna blame us Americlaps for clapping in Japan? That's going too far.

[]: Post by michaelovan » Sat Nov 17, 2012 1:24 pm Der Kommissar wrote: We're gonna blame us Americlaps for clapping in Japan?
